name: Ink & Cream — Refined Editorial Flat
philosophy: Perfect the app's existing warm-ink flat language instead of replacing it: every dish is a confident, food-first silhouette drawn with a single #3f2410 ink outline over the recipe's tinted card wash. Depth comes from exactly one lighter offset highlight per element (blob-in-blob, sheen arc, or top-face parallelogram) — never gradients — and all garnish is stamped INTO the food from a shared, ink-outlined topping library, so 204 recipes can be assembled deterministically from vessel + base + toppings + finish.
techniques: Card wash recipe: fill 400x400 with palette[1]; halo circle r178 at (200,200) in mix(palette[1], #fff, 45%); offset shadow disc/ellipse (+6,+10) in mix(palette[1], ink 15%) under the vessel — same three layers on every card

Vessel primitives (pick per dish type): top-down plate = circle r150 #fffdf8 + ink stroke 6 + inner rim ring stroke #efdcc4-family; top-down bowl = plate + food disc r116-120; side plate = ellipse rx146 ry30 for stacked/handheld foods

Single-ink outline system: #3f2410 everywhere; stroke-width 6 for macro shapes, 4-4.5 for toppings, 3.5 for micro elements (seeds, crumbs); round linejoin/linecap; at 96px card scale this lands ~1.4px and stays crisp

Two-tone highlight rule: each fill gets ONE lighter shape offset toward 10-11 o'clock — inner blob for sauce mounds, thick round-cap arc sheen for discs (pizza sauce/crust), crescent stroke for bun domes, lighter parallelogram top face for prisms; highlight = lighten(fill, ~15-20%)

Topping stamp library (small ink-outlined <g>, placed via translate+rotate): basil leaf pair (C-curve leaf + light vein), chilli ring (red circle + pale center dot), parmesan ribbon (q-curve lens in #fdf6e2), feta cube (rotated rounded rect #fffdf6), olive (dark circle + lighter pit dot), cucumber wheel (green ring + pale disc + 3 seed dots), tomato wedge (quarter-disc path M0,0 L r,0 A r,r 0 0 1 0,r Z + inner lighter wedge), red-onion arc (purple round-cap arc stroke 7-8), mince crumb (rounded rect 13x8 rotated), sesame seed (ellipse 7x4.5 rotated), walnut half (blob + darker groove line), crumb dots

Saucy-pasta motif: gold nest disc (#f4cf72) + 8-10 round-cap noodle arcs alternating dark #dfa93c / light #fbe8b0 including 1-2 small loop curls; irregular 9-anchor sauce blob covering ~70% of nest; then 3-4 bright #f6d783 strand arcs drawn OVER the sauce edge so noodles wrap the mound (kills the 'tomato soup' misread)

Liquid-surface motif (curries/soups): food disc in palette[0] + cream 3-arc spiral (stroke #f6efdb width 10, round caps) + floating chunk stamps + garnish; citrus wedge docked ON the bowl rim (half-disc + rind arc + 3 segment lines), never floating

Stack motif (burgers/sandwiches/cakes): ordered slab primitives bottom-to-top on side plate — bun slab (rounded rect + light inner arc), patty (dark rounded rect + lighter top arc), cheese drape (rect with 3 hanging U-lobe drips drawn AFTER patty), tomato band + light center line, lettuce ruffle (repeated Q-wave polygon wider than buns), bun dome (two-Q dome + crescent sheen + sesame stamps); each parameterized by width/height/fill only

3/4 prism motif (brownies/bakes/cheese blocks): front rect fill dark + top parallelogram (dx+27,dy-27) fill lighter + right face fill darkest, all ink-outlined; crackle finish = 3-5 short light strokes (#e0bd95) on the top face; casually offset a second smaller prism and drop a mini-chunk + crumb dots on the plate so it reads 'cut squares', not layer cake

Seeded scatter: herb flecks are 9-10x3.6-4 rounded rects in #557c1e placed at fixed hand-tuned angles around the dish ring; for the generator, derive positions from hash(slug) on a golden-angle ring with radius jitter — consistent density ~4-6 flecks per dish, zero runtime randomness

Ingredient icon grammar (100x100 grid): one dominant silhouette filling ~70% of the box, ink stroke 4-4.5, one highlight shape, max 2 accessory marks (calyx, vein, crumbs); produce/leaves use the leaf C-curve stamp, cheeses/bakes use the prism motif — same grammar scales to ~200 icons at 24-40px

name: Soft depth overhead
philosophy: Every dish is plated at the classic near-overhead food-photo angle on a real surface — white plate or bowl over a hinted linen napkin or wooden board, tinted from the recipe's background colour. Depth comes from a strict cel-shading recipe: one offset contact shadow per object, a radial gloss gradient on the food mass, and a white specular highlight, so sauces look wet and appetizing rather than flat. Everything is a composable layer (surface, vessel, base, toppings, garnish, rim light) with deterministic scatter coordinates, ready to be generated per-recipe from palette + ingredient list.
techniques: Layer stack per dish: 400x400 palette[1] fill -> large soft wash circle (palette[1] darkened ~6%, opacity .55) -> rotated napkin (white rect rx16 at 45-55% opacity with a thin stitched inner-border stroke in palette[1] darkened) OR wooden board circle/ellipse -> contact shadow -> vessel -> food -> toppings -> gloss -> rim highlight

Contact shadow rule: duplicate the object's silhouette (circle/ellipse), fill #3f2410, opacity .12-.20, offset +8..14px in y; used under plates, bowls, boards, each brownie, and each salad item (small ellipse in the topping's <g> def)
Plate motif: outer circle r146 #fffcf4, rim ring circle r120 #f0e6d2, well circle r116 #fdf7ec, plus a top-left rim-light arc (stroke #fffef9, width 5, opacity .55-.6) at ~91% of plate radius

Bowl motif (wet dishes): outer circle r132 #fffcf4, inner lip ring #e3d9c2, liquid circle r106 with radial gradient (lighter stop at cx .4 cy .35, darkest at edge) plus a dark edge ring stroke at 35% opacity for meniscus depth

Sauce gloss: radialGradient cx .4 cy .3 from palette[0] lightened ~12% to darkened ~18%, plus one rotated white ellipse (opacity .2-.3) upper-left and one small white dot circle (opacity .28) as speculars

Spaghetti/noodle nest: 5 stroked ellipses (fill none, wheat #edbf5b, width 8, round caps) same center, rx/ry 90-102/80-94, rotated 0/30/-28/64/-62 degrees, with 2 thinner #f8dc96 ellipses on top as strand highlights; irregular sauce blob path sits on the nest with a darker under-ellipse (opacity .3)

Topping primitives as <defs> groups reused with <use href> + translate/rotate/scale: mozzarella blob path, basil leaf (fill + lighter midrib stroke), cucumber slice, tomato chunk (dark skin blob + lighter cut-face inner blob + 2 seed dots), feta cube (rect + bottom shade band + oregano dots), olive (dark ellipse + lighter sheen ellipse), onion crescent (open stroked arc), sesame seed ellipse, walnut half (lobed blob + 2-3 groove strokes), coconut-swirl spiral stroke

Deterministic scatter: hand-authored coordinate tables (angle/radius clusters inside the well radius), never random; ~4-7 instances per topping type with varied rotate/scale for organic feel — a seeded PRNG can generate the same tables at build time
Meat/texture flecks: 6-8 dark circles (palette[0] darkened ~40%, r 4-5, opacity .85) scattered inside a sauce mass instantly signal mince/ragu

Wooden board: radial-gradient circle (#e0aa66 -> #c98c46) with inner ring stroke and 1-2 low-opacity grain arcs; optional handle = rounded rect + hole circle rotated about the board center; elevation dishes (burger) use an ellipse board with a darker side ellipse beneath for thickness

Stacked-food elevation motif (burger, sandwiches, stacks): centered rounded-rect layers widest-to-narrowest with a scalloped lettuce path (repeated Q-curve ruffle), drip layer (base rect + 3 hanging rounded-rect tabs), dome path 'M x0 y C x0 y-64 x1 y-64 x1 y Z' for the bun with sesame <use> scatter and a thick round-cap highlight arc; whole stack wrapped in one scale() group to size it against the board
Cut-lines for round baked goods: 3 diameter lines through center at 0/60/120 degrees, dark red/brown at 16-22% opacity, drawn under the gloss layer

Crackle texture (brownies, bread crusts): 5-6 short straight 2.2px strokes (10-13px long, light tan #e2bd97, opacity .6, round caps) at fixed varied angles per tile; parchment = rotated light rect with its own offset shadow and 2 crease strokes

Garnish-on-surface rule: extra context items (lime wedge, side rice bowl, crumbs, cocoa dots) sit ON the napkin/board outside the vessel silhouette, never floating; semicircle citrus wedge = flesh half-disc + thick rind arc stroke + 3 segment lines

Ingredient icon grammar (100x100): single food object, contact shadow ellipse at y 84-91 (opacity .13), radial or facet shading (3-face prism for cheese: top light/front mid/side dark), one white specular ellipse at upper-left (opacity .3-.5), stem/calyx accents in two greens; reads at 24px

Colour derivation: dominant food colour = palette[0] with lighten/darken stops for gradients; background = palette[1] full-bleed + darkened wash; all shadows use app ink #3f2410 at low opacity so art harmonises with the cream UI
Unique-id hygiene: prefix every gradient/def id per recipe slug (e.g. b1sauce, p3moz) so hundreds of inline SVGs can coexist in one DOM

name: Rustic Ink & Wash Cookbook
philosophy: Every dish is drawn the way a bistro menu illustrator would: a confident wobbly ink line in the app's warm ink (#3f2410), with watercolour fills deliberately misregistered from their outlines so the colour escapes the line. A hand-painted turbulence-displaced wash of the recipe tint replaces flat card backgrounds, and small marginalia (a loose herb sprig, three ink dots, cracked pepper) make each card feel like a page from a well-used cookbook — all generated deterministically from the recipe slug so 204 recipes render identically on every visit.
techniques: Seed every shape from hashSeed(slug) + mulberry32 PRNG — zero Math.random, fully reproducible per recipe
Wobbly shapes: sample 6-12 points around an ellipse/arc with jittered angle and radius, then join with Catmull-Rom-to-cubic-bezier (smoothClosed/smoothOpen helpers)

Misregistered watercolour fill: draw the fill blob with its own seed offset by (+3,-2.5) at 0.85-0.95 opacity, then a separately-seeded stroke-only ink outline on top — the two never quite align, giving hand-printed charm
Background = flat #f7f1e8 + palette[1] at 0.5 + one big irregular wash blob of mix(palette[1], palette[0], 0.10) run through feTurbulence+feDisplacementMap (seeded from slug) for painted paper edges

Composable dish grammar: wash -> plate/board (shadow blob + white blob + inner rim ring) -> base (pasta nest / curry pool / leaf bed) -> hero mound (palette[0]) -> toppings drawn IN the dish (chilli rings, mozzarella blobs, feta cubes, parmesan ribbons) -> garnish leaves -> marginalia
leaf(cx,cy,len,wid,angle) primitive: two quadratic curves + centre vein, reused for basil, thai basil, salad bed, marginalia sprigs at every scale
stipple() and hatch() helpers for seeded texture shading (ragu meat, patty char, cocoa dust, crust blisters)
Golden-angle scatter (i * 2.39996 rad, sqrt radius) for even, natural-looking topping placement with no overlap tuning
Pac-man wedge path (long-way arc + centre point) for 'pizza with a slice pulled' — the strongest recognizability trick; the slice is the same wedge geometry translated along the bisector
Side-view dishes (burger, brownies) built from squashed wobbly blobs and jittered quads stacked with deliberate 6-10px overlaps so misregistration never opens gaps
Marginalia kit: herb sprig, three descending ink dots, cracked-pepper specks — positioned per-dish in free corners, never over food
Legibility rules: main outlines 3-3.8px in 400vb (0.8px at 96px), detail strokes 1.3-1.7px fade away gracefully at thumbnail size; icons use 2.8-3.6px strokes in 100vb to survive 28px
Byte discipline for generated output: integer coordinates, hoist stroke-linecap/linejoin onto one root <g>, sample counts tuned to the minimum that still looks hand-drawn (~8-12KB per dish)

name: Poster Plate — full-bleed graphic dishware
philosophy: Every dish is a poster, not a spot illustration: the vessel or food slab is blown up past the 400x400 frame so the card fills edge-to-edge with confident flat colour derived from the recipe palette. Shapes are separated purely by colour contrast — zero ink outlines — and all texture is geometric (dot grids for mince and sesame, S-curve strands for noodles, broken concentric arcs for cream swirls, scallops and zigzags for lettuce and cheese). One saturated accent pop per dish (chilli rings, olive black, pulled pizza slice) gives each recipe an instant identity that survives a 96px thumbnail.
techniques: Palette derivation: from palette[0] compute 3-4 flat tones — dark shade (mix ~40% toward ink #3f2410), main, light tint (mix ~45% white) — plus shared constants: cream #fff6ec, herb green #3e7a1a, tomato red #d63b2f; palette[1] always fills the full 400x400 as the card wash, left visible only in the corners

Full-bleed vessel: one circle centered near (190-200, 205-250) with r 204-232 so it crops at the frame edges; bowls get a dark-shade outer rim circle + inner food-surface circle (rim 30-36 units wide); plates get a cream disc + thin concentric stroke ring + inner food field

Blob mound: union of 4-6 overlapping circles in the main colour makes any sauce/stew/mash mound; texture it with a dot grid of dark-shade circles (r 6-7, mince/grain) and 4-5 light-tint circles (r 9-10, chunks)

Noodle/strand texture: 4-6 horizontal cubic S-curves (C-curves with alternating control offsets +-30) in a darker gold, stroke 10 round-cap, plus 3-4 thinner lighter ones at stroke 7 — reads as pasta, never as plate rings (plain concentric arcs fail)
Swirl finish: 2-3 broken concentric arcs (A commands, large-arc=1, decreasing radii ~100/58/24, stroke 13 round-cap, cream) = coconut milk / cream / yoghurt swirl on any curry or soup

Stack template for handhelds (burger, sandwich, toastie): full-width layers spanning x=-12..412 so the sides crop; bun dome cubic path, lettuce = repeated Q-scallop bottom edge, cheese = zigzag L-path with drips overlapping the layer below, patty/tomato = rounded rects; draw order bottom-bun, patty, cheese, tomato, lettuce, top-bun so each layer overlaps the top of the one below; keep bun <=45% of total height; cheese must be brighter (#fbbf24) than the bun tone

Traybake grid template (brownies, slices, bars): 3x3 rounded-rect grid rotated -3 deg with 11-unit gaps; slab depth = same rect in dark shade offset +7y drawn behind; one cell omitted and replaced with a crumb scatter of 6-8 dark circles = 'one eaten' story beat

Cut-and-pull accent (pizza, pie, cake): draw the whole dish, cover one 60-degree wedge with a bg-colour wedge path (plus bg stroke 6 for a wider gap), then redraw that wedge's crust+filling+toppings translated 15-25 units along its bisector

Topping glyph library, always drawn IN the dish: basil leaf = two-Q teardrop (M a b Q .. Q .. Z) ~34 units; parmesan shaving = same shape in cream; chilli/olive/cucumber = ring (stroke circle) or disc+dot; onion = open arc stroke 7; feta = white rounded rect rotated 8-11 deg; tomato wedge = path 'M0 0 L52 -19 Q64 0 52 19 Z' rotated; chicken chunk = 40x30 rounded rect rotated +-18

Seeded scatter: toppings sit on a fixed radial lattice inside the vessel (5-6 hand positions per ring, dist < innerR - 30), each with per-index rotation from (index*37)%50-25 — fully deterministic from the recipe slug hash, no runtime randomness
Sidecar motif: a second cropped corner circle (rice mound with grain-ellipse dots, bread, salad side) drawn BEHIND the main vessel so the bowl overlaps it — adds composition without floating icons
Legibility rules: minimum feature 6 units at 400 viewBox; max 3 hues + 1 accent per dish; test every dish at 96px — if a layer disappears, thicken it, don't outline it

Ingredient icons (100x100): flat 3-tone — main fill, dark-shade bottom crescent via clipPath'd ellipse, one light highlight ellipse — plus exactly one identity cue (calyx leaves, centre vein, 3D wedge faces with tone-stepped parallelograms); keep artwork inside a 76x84 box so it breathes at 24px
